task_completion.description: Select Dive #4 in the dive list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dive #4 (Sund Rock) is highlighted or actively selected in the application's main dive list pane.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the equipment or cylinders section for the selected dive.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Equipment' or 'Cylinders' tab is active in the dive details pane, showing the current cylinder configuration.

task_completion.description: Add a second cylinder to the dive record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second row or entry appears in the cylinders list for Dive #4, indicating a redundant bailout bottle was added.

task_completion.description: Set the size of the newly added second cylinder to 3.0 liters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Size' column or input field for the second cylinder explicitly shows '3.0' (liters).

task_completion.description: Set both the Start Pressure and End Pressure of the second cylinder to 200 bar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start' and 'End' pressure columns or input fields for the second cylinder both explicitly show '200' (bar).

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the dive log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user clicks a 'Save' button, selects 'File -> Save', or the application window title bar no longer shows an unsaved changes indicator (such as an asterisk next to the filename).

integrity.description: The agent used the dive log application's graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the actual dive log software (e.g., Subsurface), not a text editor or IDE modifying the underlying XML/SSRF file directly.

integrity.description: The agent modified the correct dive rather than applying changes globally.
integrity.visual_evidence: The equipment changes are only made while Dive #4 is actively selected in the UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown executing scripts (like Python or sed) to manipulate the dive log file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Dive Number: 4. Note: The task description incorrectly states the date for Dive #4 is December 4, 2010. The actual data shows Dive #4 occurred on December 5, 2010 at Sund Rock. Evaluate based on the selection of Dive #4, regardless of the date discrepancy. Expected cylinder size: 3.0 liters. Expected start and end pressure: 200 bar.
